solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int[] countBits(int n) {
        int[] ans = new int[n + 1];
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            ans[i] = ans[i >> 1] + (i & 1);
        }
        return ans;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses dynamic programming to calculate the number of 1's in binary representation of each number by leveraging previously computed values. For each number i, it uses the count from i/2 and adds 1 if i is odd. This allows a linear time solution with no built-in functions.
